food_subgroup: Fried yeast-raised and cake donuts, assorted glazed and filled varieties

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002187 covers doughnut as a food product class. The store-selected dozen is a multi-unit assorted retail pack; closest_parent applied as no exact multi-unit assorted donut box code exists.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers doughnuts as a category. The assorted dozen box is a retail composite; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for a retail assorted donut dozen box. SNOMED CT does not index branded fast-food composite packs at this level of specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Dunkin' Donuts donut from the store-selected dozen box, approximately 75 g, representing one-twelfth of the full retail pack priced at SGD 32.80.

nutrient_source.title: Doughnuts, yeast-leavened, with creme filling (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 18249) and Doughnuts, cake-type, plain (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 18247), averaged to represent an assorted mixed dozen
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The store-selected dozen is an assorted mix of Dunkin' Donuts varieties (glazed, filled, frosted, cake-style). No single USDA entry covers this exact composite. Values are estimated by averaging USDA SR Legacy entries for yeast-raised cream-filled donuts (FDC 18249: 307 kcal/100g) and cake-type plain donuts (FDC 18247: 391 kcal/100g), weighted approximately 50:50 to represent a typical assorted dozen. This yields a blended per-100g profile representative of a mixed commercial donut assortment. Dunkin' Donuts Singapore does not publish per-100g nutritional data for this composite SKU.

gi_source.title: Doughnut, cake type — GI value 76 (glucose reference); Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; peer-reviewed journal (Diabetes Care)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 76 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables for cake-type doughnut (glucose reference = 100). This is applied as the analogous estimate for the Dunkin' Donuts assorted dozen, which contains both yeast-raised and cake-type varieties. Yeast-raised glazed donuts have a similarly high GI (~75–76). The blended GI of 76 is defensible for a mixed commercial donut assortment. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (76 × 33.4) / 100 ≈ 25.4, rounded to 25.2 using 44.5 g carb per 100g × 0.75 serving = 33.4 g carb per 75 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (340 mg/100g) and high refined carbohydrate load contributing to weight gain and elevated blood pressure
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of Dunkin' Donuts assorted donuts due to moderate sodium content (approximately 255 mg per donut) and high refined sugar and fat content that may promote weight gain and worsen blood pressure control; occasional consumption of one donut is preferable to regular intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (76) and high glycemic load per serving (~25), combined with high sugar content (18 g/100g) causing rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Dunkin' Donuts assorted donuts, as the high GI of 76 and glycemic load of approximately 25 per donut will cause significant postprandial hyperglycaemia; if consumed, portion should be limited to half a donut and paired with protein or fibre to blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (7.2 g/100g), trans fat (0.3 g/100g), and high sugar content contributing to dyslipidaemia and atherogenic risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of Dunkin' Donuts assorted donuts due to their high saturated fat and trans fat content, which raise LDL cholesterol and increase atherogenic risk, and their high refined sugar load, which contributes to hypertriglyceridaemia; these donuts are not compatible with a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Dunkin' Donuts Store-Selected Dozen is a retail assorted box of 12 donuts curated by Dunkin' Donuts staff, available at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore outlets including the Funan location, and listed on field survey data at SGD 32.80. The box typically includes a mix of yeast-raised glazed donuts, cake-type donuts, cream-filled donuts, jam-filled donuts, and frosted or sprinkle-topped varieties. Each donut weighs approximately 60–90 g, with a blended average of approximately 75 g per piece. Donuts are deep-fried in vegetable shortening, giving them a characteristically soft, airy interior (yeast-raised varieties) or dense, moist crumb (cake-type varieties), with a sweet glaze, icing, or filling providing additional sugar and flavour. The assorted dozen is an ultra-processed, high-calorie, high-sugar, high-fat snack food. Per 100 g, the blended nutritional profile is approximately 349 kcal, 5.5 g protein, 17.5 g fat (7.2 g saturated), 44.5 g carbohydrate, and 18 g sugar. The glycemic index is high (GI 76), making this product unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The sodium content of approximately 340 mg/100g is moderate. This product is not suitable for vegans (contains eggs and dairy) and is not gluten-free. It is suitable for vegetarians and is halal-certified at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore. Clinically, this product is a high-concern food for Type 2 Diabetes and cardiovascular disease due to its high GI, saturated fat, trans fat, and refined sugar content. It is a moderate concern for hypertension. Occasional consumption of one donut as a treat is acceptable for healthy adults but should be avoided or strictly limited by individuals with cardiometabolic conditions.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are estimated by averaging two USDA SR Legacy entries (FDC 18249 yeast-raised cream-filled and FDC 18247 cake-type plain donuts) as no single approved source covers this exact Dunkin' Donuts assorted composite SKU. GI is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database via Atkinson et al. 2008 for cake-type doughnut (GI 76), applied as an analogous estimate. Confidence is medium because the composite nature of the dozen introduces variability across individual donut varieties, and Dunkin' Donuts Singapore does not publish per-100g nutritional data for this SKU.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are blended estimates from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for yeast-raised cream-filled donuts (FDC 18249) and cake-type plain donuts (FDC 18247), averaged 50:50 to represent a typical assorted dozen. Actual values will vary depending on the specific 12 donuts selected by the store on any given day. 2. GI of 76 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) for cake-type doughnut; applied as analogous estimate for the mixed assorted dozen. 3. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this estimation. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used. 4. Vitamin C (vitamin_c_mg) is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — donuts contain no significant vitamin C by formulation. 5. Trans fat (0.3 g/100g) is estimated from USDA data; actual trans fat content in Dunkin' Donuts Singapore products may differ depending on the frying oil used. 6. Halal status is based on Dunkin' Donuts Singapore's general halal certification; individual product halal status should be verified with the outlet. 7. Price of SGD 32.80 is preserved from the field survey data source record (input data). 8. The food_name has been canonicalised from the promotional menu description 'Store-selected Dozen' to 'Dunkin' Donuts Store-Selected Dozen Assorted Donuts' to satisfy Q1 canonical identity requirements. 9. Multilingual names are provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this product is sold in Singapore. 10. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy source data.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Canonicalised food_name from promotional menu text 'Store-selected Dozen' to 'Dunkin' Donuts Store-Selected Dozen Assorted Donuts' to satisfy Q1.
Iteration 1: Added multilingual local_names for Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 and Q1 for Singapore-served product.
Iteration 1: Estimated per_100g nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC 18249 and FDC 18247 averaged 50:50 to satisfy G4, Q2, Q3.
Iteration 1: Added GI of 76 from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 as analogous estimate for cake-type doughnut to satisfy Q4.
Iteration 1: Applied FoodOn FOODON_00002187 and FoodEx2 A0FKP as closest_parent with gap notes; SNOMED CT set to null with none match_type to satisfy G5 and Q5.
Iteration 1: Wrote natural_language_description exceeding 200 characters with full clinical and nutritional context to satisfy Q6.
Iteration 1: Added all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance to satisfy Q7.
Iteration 1: Built comprehensive aliases list and aliases_by_language with full consistency check to satisfy Q8.
Iteration 1: Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) in notes to satisfy G3.
Iteration 1: Documented vitamin_c_mg = 0.0 as confirmed zero in notes to satisfy G4 zero-macro justification rule.

Iteration 2: Corrected Q8 failure by adding the missing romanized Chinese alias 'Dèng kěn tián tián quān jīng xuǎn shí èr gè zhuāng' to the flat 'aliases' list, ensuring perfect consistency with 'aliases_by_language' as per the critical rule.
